label: fixed_point
text: Fixed-point notation keeps the radix point at a fixed location in machine representation.

label: fixed_point
text: Fixed-point integers are efficient for pure integer arithmetic in limited word length.

label: fixed_point
text: Fixed-point fractions place the radix point right after the sign bit.

label: fixed_point
text: Fixed-point representation is simple but has limited numeric range.

label: fixed_point
text: Two's complement fixed-point range depends on the total number of bits.

label: floating_point
text: Floating-point notation uses exponent and mantissa so the radix point can move.

label: floating_point
text: Floating-point numbers are good for representing very large and very small values.

label: floating_point
text: In floating-point format the exponent controls scale and mantissa controls precision.

label: floating_point
text: Normalized floating-point values improve precision in many operations.

label: floating_point
text: Floating-point format trades hardware complexity for a larger representable range.

label: storage_system
text: Computer storage hierarchy includes cache memory primary memory and secondary storage.

label: storage_system
text: Cache reduces the speed gap between CPU and main memory by locality.

label: storage_system
text: RAM is volatile memory while SSD and HDD are non-volatile storage devices.

label: storage_system
text: Storage hierarchy balances capacity latency and cost.

label: storage_system
text: Main memory and external storage cooperate to serve program data access.

label: bus_structure
text: Bus structure is a shared communication path among hardware components.

label: bus_structure
text: Address bus data bus and control bus are the three common system buses.

label: bus_structure
text: Address bus is usually one-way while data bus is typically two-way.

label: bus_structure
text: Buses support time-division sharing across multiple devices.

label: bus_structure
text: Microcomputer hardware interconnection is commonly based on a bus architecture.

label: controller
text: The controller fetches and decodes instructions then coordinates execution.

label: controller
text: Program Counter holds the address of the next instruction.

label: controller
text: Instruction Register stores the currently decoded instruction.

label: controller
text: The controller generates control signals for ALU memory and IO operations.

label: controller
text: Control unit handles sequencing and timing in the CPU.
